message:
Update test configuration and dependencies

Test improvements:
- badge.test.tsx: Update test assertions for accessibility
- button.test.tsx: Improve test coverage patterns

Configuration:
- apps/web/tsconfig.json: Adjust compiler options
- apps/web/package.json: Update dev dependencies
- package.json (root): Add workspace tooling updates

Update beads issue tracker state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,9 +1,15 @@ {"id":"bd-11kb","title":"Add missing cache headers to user-data API routes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T07:03:31.731604860Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T07:33:34.099566550Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T07:33:34.099272495Z","close_reason":"Added cache headers to 8 API routes: featured (public), history (private), ratings (conditional), share/mine (private), referral/stats (private), referral/code (private), share/[code] (public), status/incidents (public)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} +{"id":"bd-1eho","title":"Feature: Auto-Tagging + Duplicate Detection (Metadata Assistant)","description":"## Background As the prompt library grows, manual tagging becomes inconsistent and duplicates sneak in. Pro users need fast, reliable metadata and dedupe support. ## Scope - Auto-suggest tags, categories, and short descriptions using deterministic rules + semantic similarity (no SKILL.md). - Duplicate/near-duplicate detection with similarity thresholds and preview diffs. - Merge flow: mark as alias, merge tags, or ignore with rationale. - CLI: `jfp tags suggest ` and `jfp dedupe scan` (Pro-only). ## Deliverables - Metadata suggestion service + similarity index. - UI panel for suggestions and duplicate resolution. - CLI scan output with JSON option. - Admin override for global tag mappings. ## Test Requirements ### Unit Tests - Similarity scoring thresholds and tag suggestion rules. - Duplicate detection determinism. ### Integration Tests - Suggestions saved to prompt metadata and reflected in search. - Dedupe merge updates references (packs/workflows/collections). ### E2E Tests - Create prompt -> receive suggestions -> accept -> verify tags. - Detect duplicate -> merge -> verify references updated. ## Success Criteria - Tag consistency improves without manual rework. - Duplicate prompts are surfaced and resolvable. - Search quality improves for Pro users.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:13:48.758255166Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T14:13:48.758255166Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} +{"id":"bd-2auv","title":"Feature: Pro Cost Estimator + Budget Alerts (Web + CLI)","description":"## Background Pro users need predictable costs and time budgeting. They want to see estimated token usage and set monthly caps before running prompts. ## Scope - Deterministic token + cost estimator per prompt + model (web + CLI). - UI badges showing estimated tokens and cost; compare estimate vs actual after runs. - Budget configuration (monthly cap + per-run threshold) with alerts. - CLI: `jfp cost ` and `jfp run --estimate`, plus budget status in `jfp status`. - Prompts/prompt packs only (no SKILL.md or skills content). ## Deliverables - Shared pricing table + cost calculation utility. - UI components + settings page for budgets and alerts. - CLI commands and Pro-gated upgrade messaging. - Alert logging/audit trail for over-budget events. ## Test Requirements ### Unit Tests - Cost calculation across models and price tiers. - Budget threshold logic and alert formatting. ### Integration Tests - Estimator uses prompt variables and produces stable output. - Budget updates persist and are read by CLI. ### E2E Tests - Set budget -> estimate -> exceed -> alert shown in UI and CLI. ## Success Criteria - Estimates within acceptable tolerance vs actual token usage. - Users can see cost before running prompts. - Budget alerts prevent surprise overages.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:13:36.190844227Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T14:13:36.190844227Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-2kwr","title":"Fix support ticket rate limiting - uses in-memory Maps that reset on deploy","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-29T07:02:52.563632334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T07:23:01.666524852Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T07:23:01.666361565Z","close_reason":"Fixed by extracting rate limiting to modular lib/rate-limit module with documentation and Upstash upgrade path","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-2txr","title":"E2E Tests: History UI Components","description":"## Task Add UI-level E2E tests for the Recently Viewed History feature. ## Context The API-level E2E tests exist (e2e/history/recently-viewed.spec.ts) but UI component tests are missing. ## Test Scenarios 1. **RecentlyViewedSidebar Component** - Sidebar displays recent items - Clear history button works - Items link to correct pages - Empty state displays correctly - Loading state shows correctly 2. **History Page (/history)** - Page loads and displays items - Search/filter works - Clear all history works - Timestamps display correctly - Links navigate correctly 3. **History Tracking Integration** - Viewing a prompt adds to history - Search queries are tracked - Deduplication prevents rapid duplicates - Items appear in sidebar after viewing ## Files to Modify - e2e/history/recently-viewed.spec.ts (add UI tests) - e2e/lib/history-helpers.ts (add UI selectors) ## Success Criteria - All sidebar interactions tested - History page fully tested - Integration with tracking verified","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralStar","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:45:40.323914674Z","created_by":"CoralStar","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:54:46.005730906Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:54:46.005649042Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":13,"issue_id":"bd-2txr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation complete: - Added 16 new UI tests (34 total including API tests) - History Page UI tests: header, empty state, items display, search filter, clear button, navigation - RecentlyViewedSidebar tests: title, empty state, history link, items display, 8-item limit - History Tracking Integration tests: cross-page sync, clear sync, search query display - Updated history-helpers.ts with better UI selectors for sidebar and history page - 68 total tests (34 × 2 browser configs: chromium + Mobile Chrome)","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:54:39Z"}]} +{"id":"bd-2vsu","title":"Feature: Personalized Recommendations Feed (Pro)","description":"## Background Pro users want to discover high‑value prompts quickly without browsing the entire catalog. A personalized feed drives engagement and retention. ## Scope - "For You" feed based on usage signals (views, runs, saves) + explicit preferences. - Explanations: "Because you used X" or "Trending in your category". - Weekly digest email (opt‑in) with top recommendations. - CLI: `jfp recommend` returns a ranked list with rationale. - Prompts/prompt packs only (no SKILL.md or skills content). ## Deliverables - Ranking pipeline and data model for signals. - Feed UI with filters (timeframe, category). - Digest email template + scheduling job. - CLI command with JSON output. ## Test Requirements ### Unit Tests - Ranking weight calculations and deterministic ordering. - Preference filtering and exclusion rules. ### Integration Tests - Signal ingestion -> feed updates -> UI reflects changes. - Digest job generates consistent results. ### E2E Tests - Toggle preferences -> feed updates -> digest preview. - CLI recommend -> matches feed top items. ## Success Criteria - Users find relevant prompts faster. - Feed is explainable and feels trustworthy. - Opt‑in privacy settings are respected.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:14:02.330248162Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T14:14:02.330248162Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-3jtd","title":"EPIC: World-Class UI/UX Polish - Mobile & Desktop Optimization","description":"## Overview Hyper-optimize both mobile and desktop UI/UX to achieve world-class visual appeal, polish, and slickness following best practices from industry leaders like Stripe. ## Completed Work 1. ✅ Fixed critical mobile button text truncation in PromptDetailModal - Restructured to mobile-first approach with full-width primary action - Secondary actions in 2x2 grid that never truncates ## Mobile Optimization Goals - Ensure all touch targets are 44px minimum (iOS HIG standard) - Proper safe area insets for notched devices - Bottom-sheet interactions for modals - Optimized category filter horizontal scrolling - Touch-friendly gestures with haptic feedback ## Desktop Optimization Goals - Refined hover states and micro-interactions - Proper keyboard navigation - Consistent typography and spacing - Premium shadow and elevation design - Stripe-level polish on all interactive elements ## Components to Audit - [x] PromptDetailModal - Fixed - [x] Button component - Already well-designed - [x] BottomSheet - Well-designed with safe area support - [x] SpotlightSearch - Well-designed with mobile-first approach - [x] Nav - Good touch targets - [x] BottomNav - Good mobile design - [x] PromptCard - Good Stripe-inspired design ## Status Initial critical bug fixed. Full audit shows components are already well-designed following mobile-first principles.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-21T20:27:47.367791503Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T20:28:41.377830454Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T20:28:41.377778706Z","close_reason":"Completed UI/UX audit. Fixed critical mobile button truncation. All other components already follow mobile-first best practices with proper touch targets, safe area support, and Stripe-inspired design.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-3ttk","title":"Port jfp CLI to Rust","notes":"Phase 4 started: Created initial Rust workspace with clap-based CLI skeleton. Commands stubbed: list, search, show, categories, tags, about. Compiles successfully.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"CloudySparrow","created_at":"2026-01-21T03:10:58.492607101Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T06:36:07.925714807Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T06:36:07.924800575Z","close_reason":"Rust port complete: 21 commands implemented, all tests pass, 3.4MB binary, 2ms startup","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":15,"issue_id":"bd-3ttk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed core Rust port: Types, Storage (SQLite+FTS5), Registry, Commands (list/search/show/categories/tags/about). 13 tests passing.","created_at":"2026-01-29T04:36:08Z"}]} {"id":"bd-cry3","title":"CRITICAL: Mobile button text truncation in prompt detail modal","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-21T20:22:11.539628983Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T20:26:49.820782671Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T20:26:49.820733619Z","close_reason":"Fixed mobile button truncation - restructured to use full-width primary action + 2x2 grid for secondary actions","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} +{"id":"bd-d43s","title":"Feature: Prompt Dependency Graph + Impact Analysis","description":"## Background Prompts are reused across packs, workflows, schedules, and share links. Pro users need to understand what will break if a prompt changes. ## Scope - Maintain graph edges: prompt -> packs, workflows, schedules, share links, and collections. - Impact analysis when editing: show downstream items and required confirmations. - Graph view with filters and export. - CLI: `jfp impact ` and `jfp graph export`. - Prompts/prompt packs only (no SKILL.md or skills content). ## Deliverables - Graph storage model + API endpoints. - UI graph explorer + impact warnings. - CLI commands with JSON export. ## Test Requirements ### Unit Tests - Graph integrity rules and edge updates on edits/deletes. - Impact analysis accuracy. ### Integration Tests - Changes in prompts propagate to dependent items. - Graph export format is stable. ### E2E Tests - Edit prompt -> impact modal -> confirm -> updates applied. - CLI impact -> matches UI counts. ## Success Criteria - Users can see downstream dependencies before editing. - Accidental breaking changes are prevented.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:14:14.088091888Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T14:14:14.088091888Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} +{"id":"bd-kfuj","title":"Feature: CLI Premium Pack Subscriptions + Update Notifications","description":"## Background Pro users subscribe to premium prompt packs and expect updates to flow into their CLI without manual downloads. ## Scope - CLI pack commands: `jfp packs subscribe/list/update/changelog`. - Local cache with signed manifest and version history. - Update notifications on new pack releases; optional auto‑update. - License metadata + entitlement checks (Pro-only). - Prompts/prompt packs only (no SKILL.md or skills content). ## Deliverables - Pack subscription API endpoints + entitlement checks. - CLI flows for subscribe/update and changelog viewing. - Local cache format + integrity verification. - Upgrade messaging for free users. ## Test Requirements ### Unit Tests - Manifest verification and cache integrity. - Entitlement checks and error formatting. ### Integration Tests - Subscribe -> update -> changelog with real API calls. - Cache refresh on new pack release. ### E2E Tests - Pro user subscribes -> pack content available in CLI. - Free user attempt -> upgrade guidance. ## Success Criteria - Pro users can keep packs up to date with minimal effort. - Updates are secure and auditable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:14:29.605565630Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T14:14:29.605565630Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} +{"id":"bd-wsye","title":"Docs: Clarify CLI Free vs Pro + Build Guidance","description":"## Background Users reported that the jfp CLI does not compile and are confused about Free vs Pro gating. ## Scope - Clarify Free vs Pro command gating in the main README. - Add build guidance confirming no Pro account is required to compile. - Update CLI premium integration doc with a gating summary. - Improve bug report template for build failures. ## Completed Changes - Updated README Free vs Pro section + build steps. - Added gating summary to docs/cli-premium-integration.md. - Added CLI build diagnostics section to .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.md. ## Success Criteria - OSS users can build the CLI without Pro. - Pro-only commands are clearly communicated. - Issue reports include build details. ","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:57:57.737302847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T13:57:57.737302847Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T13:57:57.737302847Z","external_ref":"bd-pg3a","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-02u","title":"Build ChangelogAccordion for prompt detail pages","description":"**Details:** - Shows version history when prompt.changelog exists - Accordion expansion (collapsed by default) - Lists changes chronologically (newest first) - Version, date, and change description per entry **Changelog Type (in types.ts):** ```typescript interface PromptChange { version: string; date: string; changes: string[]; } // On Prompt interface: changelog?: PromptChange[]; ``` **Implementation:** ```typescript // apps/web/src/components/changelog-accordion.tsx interface ChangelogAccordionProps { changelog: PromptChange[]; } export function ChangelogAccordion({ changelog }: ChangelogAccordionProps) { const [expanded, setExpanded] = useState(false); if (!changelog?.length) return null; return (
{expanded && (
    {changelog.map((entry) => (
  • {entry.version} {entry.date}
      {entry.changes.map((change, i) => (
    • {change}
      • ))}
    • ))}
)}
); } ``` **Location:** apps/web/src/components/changelog-accordion.tsx **Reasoning:** Changelogs build trust and help users understand prompt evolution. Accordion keeps the UI clean while making history accessible.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:35:24.004177827Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T21:50:21.229495631Z","closed_at":"2026-01-10T21:50:21.229495631Z","close_reason":"Created ChangelogAccordion with collapsible UI, type-specific icons/colors, and timeline styling.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-06hp","title":"Task: Generate combined coverage report","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:49:56.733833988Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T00:48:14.075783682Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T00:48:14.075783682Z","close_reason":"Implemented merge-coverage.ts script, added coverage:all and coverage:merge scripts to package.json, updated CI to generate and upload combined coverage report","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-06hp","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-vyjo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:57:08.032982405Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-08e","title":"Implement jfp list command -- Details: Lists all prompts. Filters: --category, --tag, --featured, --limit. Human output: compact table with ID, category, tags, description. JSON output: array of prompt metadata. Auto-JSON when stdout is not TTY.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:26:47.409298404Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T06:36:30.725193679Z","closed_at":"2026-01-10T06:36:30.725193679Z","close_reason":"Added --limit flag, enhanced table output with tags, auto-JSON for non-TTY","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-08e","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-54i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:37:28.799344782Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -13,6 +13,8 @@
     "dev": "bun run --cwd apps/web dev",
     "build": "bun run --cwd apps/web build",
     "lint": "bun run --cwd apps/web lint",
+    "typecheck": "bun run --cwd apps/web typecheck",
+    "format": "bun run --cwd apps/web format",
     "test": "bun run --cwd apps/web test",
     "test:cli": "bun test jfp.test.ts packages/core/__tests__ packages/cli/__tests__",
     "test:cli:coverage": "bun test --coverage --coverage-reporter=text --coverage-reporter=lcov --coverage-dir=coverage/cli jfp.test.ts packages/core/__tests__ packages/cli/__tests__",
